content.full_text:
May 13, 1991

California Fair Political Practices Commission
General Counsel
428 J St., Ste. 800
PO Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Sirs/Madam:

Bernard Teitelbaum, a well known lobbyist, died this past week. His will has named me as executor.

I intend to assume the position of executor but waive any fees which I might claim.

Part of the business of the estate will be the temporary management and potential future sale or merger of his lobbying firm. I will be deeply involved in this question as executor.

Since I intend to waive fees is there any obvious or potential conflict of interest which arises as a result of my duties? A prompt answer would be appreciated.

Sincerely,

[Signature]

PLI:vh
